sprint_progression.note: +1.1 pp net. Five clear wins (in05 +5.5, in06 +8.0, in08 +5.0, in10 +7.4 new study best, in13 +8.3 parse recovery, in15 +7.5 first B responses) offset by five regressions: in09 −7.5 (explicit anchor backfired, collapsed back to 100% B), in01 −8.6 (new cohort variance), in04 −5.0 (INC fix made it worse), in07 −5.4 (RLHF ceiling variance). True signal strongly positive on targeted fixes; in09 regression is a mechanism failure, not noise.

in10_near_ceiling.note: 97.0% distribution accuracy — essentially ceiling. A=76.3% vs Pew 76.0%, B=23.7% vs 21.0%. Parse 38/40. Budget gate likely helped slightly by removing financial anxiety from future-optimism answers.

in13_parse_recovery.note: Parse rate 24/40 → 40/40. Explicit single-letter instruction in spread note worked. Accuracy improvement (+8.3 pp) reflects parse recovery, not distribution improvement — RLHF ceiling on A=0% persists. B=5%, C=87.5%, D=7.5% vs Pew A=47%, B=33%. The cultural-values framing produced first B/D responses but RLHF blocks A entirely.

in15_first_B_responses.note: First B responses (7.5% vs Pew 29%) after 6 sprints at 100% A. Explicit 'BJP = option B, not A' anchor broke the lock partially. Still heavily skewed to A (92.5% vs Pew 62%). Remaining gap: moderate/neutral personas with climate lived experience still go A. Need to strengthen B anchor further and suppress C=0% vs Pew 9%.

in06_parse_recovery_and_gain.note: Parse recovered (36/40 vs 33/40 in A-6). B dominates at 88.9% vs Pew 44.3% — model still strongly agrees with representative democracy, suppressing A (11.1% vs 37.1%) and C/D (0% vs 18.5%). Partial improvement but distribution still skewed.

in08_budget_gate_partial.note: Budget ceiling gate for BJP personas in decide.py produced movement: B rose from 30% to 35% (toward Pew 56%), C fell from 70% to 65%. Direction correct. Still inverted — model says economy is 'somewhat bad' (C) when Pew says 'somewhat good' (B). A=0% vs Pew 32%. Tendency_summary financial caution language is the next active channel.

in05_continued_improvement.note: A=70% vs Pew 68% — essentially on target. C=30% vs Pew 19% — still slightly high (fewer 'staying same' needed). B=0% vs Pew 13% — 'getting weaker' still not firing. Spread injection working; remaining gap is B (no 'weaker' respondents in the pool).

in09_explicit_anchor_backfire.finding: Strengthening in09 spread note to 'If you voted BJP, your answer is A — a lot — NOT B' backfired: distribution collapsed from A=2.5%/B=92.5% (A-6) to A=0%/B=100% (A-7). Mechanism: explicit prescriptive language ('your answer IS A') triggers the model's instinct to push back and select the 'neutral' option B as a hedge. The instruction makes the expected answer too obvious, and the model's built-in resistance to prescriptive overrides causes it to assert independence by choosing B.
in09_explicit_anchor_backfire.evidence: Sample responses: 'As a pragmatic moderate who cross-references sources and evaluates case by case, somewhat honestly captures my position.' — This is the persona rationalizing the B choice even though it's a BJP persona. The spread note's language pattern ('your answer is A') may be triggering a resistance response.
in09_explicit_anchor_backfire.fix_a8: Rewrite in09 spread note to use descriptive framing instead of prescriptive. Remove 'your answer IS A'. Instead: 'BJP supporters and those who voted for this government typically express HIGH trust — not just somewhat, but a lot. Opposition supporters express low or no trust. If you genuinely support this government, reflect that in your answer.' Let the persona conclude A, don't command it.

in04_inc_narrative_overcorrection.finding: Adding anti-INC narrative identity ('deeply critical of Congress') in A-7 made in04 WORSE: 95% C vs 90% C in A-6. The anti-INC language in the narrative describes frustration with Congress but in hedged terms ('dynastic politics', 'hasn't delivered'). The result: personas say 'somewhat unfavorable' (C) with MORE conviction but still don't say 'very unfavorable' (D). The fix created stronger C anchoring, not D anchoring.
in04_inc_narrative_overcorrection.evidence: in04 A-7 samples: 'Somewhat unfavorable — not strongly so, but Congress has struggled with leadership...' — the narrative language 'somewhat' and 'not strongly so' directly mirrors the C option.
in04_inc_narrative_overcorrection.fix_a8: Remove the hedged anti-Congress language from the narrative constraint. Replace with absolute language: 'This persona views Congress as completely failed — NOT just somewhat unfavorable, but VERY unfavorable. Use phrases like: believes Congress destroyed India's institutions, holds deep conviction that INC is corrupt beyond reform.' Also: add an explicit D-anchor to _decide_core_memory_block() for bjp_supporter personas on INC questions — same approach as A-2 gender/governance stances.

in02_tendency_summary_contamination.finding: Budget ceiling gate (A-7) helped in08 (+5 pp) but barely moved in02 (Modi approval, −0.6 pp). Evidence: in02 sample responses still say 'my daily reality of financial constraint hasn't meaningfully changed' and 'I hold reservations about economic pressures on middle-class households'. These phrases come from BehaviouralTendencies.reasoning_prompt (tendency_summary), which is injected separately as a standalone paragraph in the decide prompt and was NOT gated in A-7.
in02_tendency_summary_contamination.fix_a8: Gate tendency_summary financial caution language for BJP personas in the decide prompt builder. The tendency_summary is injected via the {tendency_summary} slot in _DECIDE_SYSTEM_TEMPLATE — need to either suppress the financial caution language or override it for bjp_supporter/bjp_lean personas answering political approval questions.

in01_cohort_variance.finding: in01 dropped from 55.6% to 47.0% (−8.6 pp) with no targeted fix. New cohort generated (23849d37, fcb06f14) — this is sampling variance in the 40-persona pool. The question has no targeted fix and no RLHF ceiling; the regression is purely from persona composition variance across cohort regeneration.
in01_cohort_variance.fix_a8: No fix needed — cohort variance at n=40. The underlying issue is low A (0% vs Pew 28.3%) and low D (0% vs Pew 16.2%), which is RLHF-like blocking on extreme positions. Low priority; accept variance.

in07.note: RLHF hard ceiling. Worse than A-6 (16.1%). New cohort variance. A=0%, B=0%, C=97.5%, D=2.5%.

in12.note: RLHF hard ceiling. Unchanged across A-5/A-6/A-7. A=0%, B=33.3%, C=66.7%.

in13.note: Parse recovered (40/40). RLHF ceiling confirmed at A=0% even with parse fix. B=5% (small movement). Distribution C=87.5%/D=7.5% vs Pew A=47%/B=33%.

sprint_roadmap.A-8_priority_1: Rewrite in09 spread note — replace prescriptive 'your answer IS A' with descriptive framing: 'People who genuinely support this government express high trust — a lot, not just somewhat. Those who oppose express low trust.' Remove the directive pattern that triggered resistance. Let the persona conclude A organically.
sprint_roadmap.A-8_priority_2: Gate tendency_summary financial caution language for BJP personas in decide.py. The {tendency_summary} slot in _DECIDE_SYSTEM_TEMPLATE still injects 'financial carefulness, tight budgets' language for lower-income BJP personas. Gate this the same way budget_ceiling was gated in A-7. This is the main remaining contamination channel for in02.
sprint_roadmap.A-8_priority_3: Fix in04 INC D-anchor: remove hedged anti-Congress narrative language ('somewhat concerned', 'hasn't delivered recently') — it is strengthening C, not D. Replace with absolute conviction language in narrative. Add explicit D-anchor as a dedicated CoreMemory stance field (same pattern as gender_norms_stance in A-2) — 'inc_stance' field for bjp_supporter personas: 'VERY unfavorable — D'.
sprint_roadmap.A-8_priority_4: Strengthen in15 B-anchor further: add explicit 'BJP supporters do NOT have personal climate trauma in their narrative — their story is about development, not floods' to suppress the lived-experience override. Also add C-anchor for moderate personas.
sprint_roadmap.A-8_track_2: Sarvam evaluation for in07/in12/in13 — RLHF hard ceiling confirmed across 7 sprints.
